title: Cyperus sumbawangensis
taxonomy: Cyperus sumbawangensis is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family includes a large number of plant species, many of which are aquatic or semi-aquatic. Cyperaceae is a part of the larger monocot clade, which is characterized by having one cotyledon in the seedling stage. The order within which Cyperaceae is classified is Poales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in wet environments. Cyperus sumbawangensis itself is likely classified within the genus Cyperus, which comprises a wide range of sedge species.

intro: Cyperus sumbawangensis, also known as Sumbawa flatsedge, is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus sumbawangensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyperus sumbawangensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in topical applications to relieve pain and inflammation.
